title: tomophyllum subrepandulum
intro: Tomophyllum subrepandulum, also known as the creeping maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Tomophyllum subrepandulum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, making it a valuable component in natural medicine practices.
